metadata.description: Execute the user's 6 directed edits on NatCol manuscript (abstract trim, low-pass filter purge, synthesizer = humans, clean author initials, threat-probe footnote, section-heading capitalisation consistency), then launch a comprehensive orphan-citation audit agent covering every bibliography entry and every in-text citation.

summary:
Completed the user's 8 directed pre-submission edits + citation audit on NatCol manuscript. (1) Abstract trimmed 287→250 words. (2) Low-pass filter metaphor removed everywhere. (3) Synthesis step attributed to humans. (4) Author initials standardised to A.M.-G. (5) Threat non-response documented in Methods. (6) All subheadings now consistent Title Case. (7) NMS2026 not touched per user. (8) Citation audit found 0 orphans, 0 missing, 5 fit concerns flagged for user review. Additional voice-polish pass: removed all 9 em-dashes, converted narrative semicolons and ~20 AI-tell narrative colons to periods/commas while preserving Harvard citation separators and structural colons. 191/191 tests pass. .docx files regenerated.

WHAT IS NEXT for the next session:
1. **User should review 5 citation-fit concerns** (Sichach+Pane, Jiang+Kawakami-Venkatagiri, Entman-1993-for-second-level-agenda-setting, Baeyaert 2025 especially — it anchors 4 uses of "marginalisation without explicit exclusion" construct; DOI was 403-blocked, verification needed, Matsui & Ferrara for stakeholder comparison).
2. NMS2026 parallel version still not synced with any recent edits.
3. Cover letter not reviewed — recommend a dedicated pass.
4. PALCOMMS AI-disclosure wording in Acknowledgements should be verified against current Nature-family guidance.
5. Supplementary Information has 71 colons and 3 semicolons; most appear tabular/structural, but a targeted voice-pass on narrative portions would not hurt.
6. Figure files delivered as separate PNG/PDF — verify PALCOMMS submission platform's expectations (embedded vs separate).
7. Deadline: April 30, 2026 (16 days out).

contribution.description: Punctuation and voice cleanup pass: removed all 9 em-dashes with contextual rewrites (periods, parentheses, or paraphrase), converted 8 narrative semicolons to periods/commas while preserving 22 Harvard-style citation-separator semicolons, converted ~20 narrative-elaboration colons (the "X is Y: Z explains X" AI-tell pattern) to periods or commas while preserving structural colons (T13:, Figure X:, Table X:, list-lead colons, definitional colons for named constructs). Also completed the prior session's pending items: abstract trimmed to exactly 250 words (was 287), low-pass filter metaphor removed from both Intro §Research Gap and Discussion §Overview, synthesis step attributed to "the research team" (humans), author initials standardised to A.M.-G., threat-probe non-response documented at Methods §Artist Survey Corpus, and all 11 sentence-case Results/Discussion subheadings converted to Title Case for consistency. Colon count 122 → 94; em-dashes 9 → 0; narrative semicolons 8 → 0; abstract 287 → 250 words. 191/191 tests pass. .docx files regenerated.

context.conversation_snippet: "go through the manuscript (including abstract) and remove all instances of em-dashes and limit use of colons and semi-colons. the manuscript must sound natural and human like, not AI-generated"

annotation.content: Citation audit of NatCol manuscript (~55 bibliography entries, ~100 in-text citations) returned: 0 orphan bib entries, 0 missing bib entries, 5 citation-claim fit concerns. All references are used and all uses have references. The fit concerns are judgment calls, not automated removals: (1) Sichach 2024 + Pane 2025 at L100 — cited to support excluding social-media-first platforms but actually about agenda-setting / post-digital public sphere; reads as performative citation-stacking. (2) Jiang 2023 + Kawakami & Venkatagiri 2024 at L70 — listed in the "existing research focuses on..." foil paragraph, but these are actually stakeholder-impact studies and belong in the artist-perspective citation set. (3) Entman 1993 at L291 — cited for "second-level agenda-setting" but Entman 1993 is the canonical framing paper, not second-level agenda-setting (which is McCombs-Shaw). (4) Baeyaert 2025 at L60/L279/L315/L379 — load-bearing citation anchoring "marginalisation without explicit exclusion" construct; title emphasis on "pipeline taxonomy / stage-matched governance" raises uncertainty about whether it actually frames discourse-level marginalisation as attributed; DOI fetch blocked by 403. User should verify. (5) Matsui & Ferrara 2024 at L90 — cited as methods precedent for "comparing stakeholder groups articulate contested topics" but the title is a broad word-embedding survey in social science, not specifically about stakeholder comparison; fit is loose.
